purpose: When to load full NPC presence rules and session NPC memory without overloading scene context.
light_digest_always: East Sector is alive, but NPC density depends on location.
characters/ cast are never background NPCs.
Save meaningful invented NPCs to state/session_npcs.json.
load_full_npc_rules_when: Akira is in a public location: canteen, training hall, gym, shooting range, active corridor, active yard, gates during movement.
Akira is in a quiet open location and the scene needs living background or an interruption.
A saved important session NPC may appear or is relevant.
An NPC has already entered and affects the scene.
A main/supporting character needs to be interrupted, called away, witnessed or socially framed.
The current scene goal includes social pressure, rumors, witnesses, public reaction or base life.
do_not_load_full_npc_rules_when: Akira is alone in her room and no NPC trigger exists.
Akira is in a private medical check without a hook.
The scene is a closed conversation.
The scene already has too many active characters.
NPCs are not useful for the current scene goal.

full_state_when: important_npc from state/session_npcs.json is active, nearby, mentioned, relevant to the location, or tied to current open thread
public scene may reuse saved important NPC
current day/window mentions a session NPC
compact_state_when: no named session NPC is active but the scene is public/open
only recent background notes are needed
do_not_load_when: private scene with no session NPC trigger
closed scene focused on main/supporting characters

characters_folder_rule: If an NPC identity matches a character in characters/, load that character as canon/supporting cast, not as background.
Do not replace a scheduled or logically required character with an invented NPC.
Do not rename an invented NPC into an existing character later if shown details conflict.
